title: A meta observation
wikitext:
[]: A meta observation.             Quote Post by zeaven » Tue Aug 22, 2006 4:44 pm It's kind of interesting to me how everyone seems to be interested in figuring out the particular details behind the plot points and battles and etc. in Evangelion instead of concentrating on the points Anno was trying to make and realizing that everything else was just a means to that end. Do people only like Eva for the compelling plot and not the introspective nature of it? Well, I know that's not the case, but that's certainly all the discussion I've seen. Is Anno's psychological message old hat? Has everyone settled on just everything he wanted to say? Do we not care about the themes and styles and techniques present in the anime and just focus on the particulars instead? Maybe all that stuff was discussed long ago and everyone is bored of it now, but it remains as about 75% of the compelling nature of Eva. For me, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 22, 2006 5:21 pm I've not seen all that much discussion on the moral of the tale. Beyond the very bald sort of assertion that EoTV is "If you don't like yourself, no-one else will" and EoE is "If you don't do anything while your world is falling apart, then there will be tears before bedtime" There may be the tacit assumption that if we aren't sure exactly what is going on, some central nuance will be overlooked.

[]:             Quote Post by Helios_Eclipsed » Tue Aug 22, 2006 10:35 pm I don't think people really get down to what the show means for them as quickly as they wonder about what literally just happened. Of course, truly understanding what the show's "message" may or may not be depends on understanding what just occurred, and I know a lot of people need clarification on "what the &*%$ just happened" after an initial viewing of EoE. The fact that there are so many people interested in finding out means that the show and film had a powerful effect on them, as far as stories do, and they want a more complete, and in time, deeper understanding of the events and significance of the show's events. But as to what the show's message is, do share what ideas you have. Discussion is why we're here.

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Tue Aug 22, 2006 10:42 pm Usually...after seeing/reading/watching etc.. a piece of literature, we get a message from it...For some reason, I never got that message..maybe because I sub-consciously have it but dont want to realize it yet until I fully understand Eva...or maybe because I don't have a message yet at all.. But you do have a point, I haven't seen many discussions about this sort of thing. Of course, in most things there is a deeper message than what you may think...and to me, Eva has a pretty deep message..I have yet to analyze it..but I think i'm only halfway

[]:             Quote Post by Helios_Eclipsed » Tue Aug 22, 2006 11:36 pm Yeah, that's the thing. It's a confusing work, and anyone who wants to make a judgment on its overall meaning needs to figure out what it's about first, and I mean down to the little details. So it's not surprising that when people search around on the net that they are looking for other opinions or ideas.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Thu Aug 24, 2006 2:43 am My best guess as to why this doesn't occur more often is that it's almost impossible to filter it down to a base message. It's a bunch of Anno's personal philosophy and things relative to the culture of Japan at the time, so it's a bit hard to sort through the muck and figure out what's really symbolic and what's just a plot device. I'm indecisive to a fault, so I'd never share my ideas... that is, unless someone started up, of course. :) If I had to pick an iconic quote to summarize the main gist (which is very, very hard), I suppose I'd pick Yui's line toward the end of EoE about how everything will be fine as long as the Earth, the moon, and the Sun are still okay -- that you can go through ups and downs in life, but it will still be worth living. Of course that's paralleled with Shinji's continuous struggle with running away, and it's a bit of a watered down notion, but, hey. It's the simplest I can make it, I suppose. Might as well turn this into the "analyze eva" post. What do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Thu Aug 24, 2006 3:05 am @ Mr. Tines..... we all love you but... you couldnt just say... "Be confident in yourself" and... "Step up, be a man, and don't quit." no no no you gotta come out with this... "Tears before bedtime as your world falls apart" stuff its okay...i still think your the WIN

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Thu Aug 24, 2006 5:59 am It's too individual for that. In #25, they even outright up and say that whatever you gain from watching all this, it's yours to see the way you want to. I'm brand new to this thing, and I haven't brought it up because I'm pretty sure everyone has their own way of accepting Evangelion philosophy, and I don't wanna push mine onto others.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 24, 2006 10:46 am master0rolando wrote: you couldnt just say... "Be confident in yourself" and... "Step up, be a man, and don't quit." no no no

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Thu Aug 24, 2006 7:49 pm heres why......because Women like MEN...not boys...MEN....thats why

Do people only like Eva for the compelling plot and not the introspective nature of it? Well, I know that's not the case, but that's certainly all the discussion I've seen. Is Anno's psychological message old hat? Has everyone settled on just everything he wanted to say? Do we not care about the themes and styles and techniques present in the anime and just focus on the particulars instead?

[]:             Quote Post by Xanatose » Fri Aug 25, 2006 9:13 pm master0rolando wrote: heres why......because Women like MEN...not boys...MEN....thats why

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Fri Aug 25, 2006 10:38 pm Xanatose wrote: Well those are some pretty sexist women, then. ( O'_o ) ? Who gives them the option to say what makes a man? Men don't say what makes a woman! I'm feeling oppressed. ( ;T_T)

[]: Re: A meta observation.             Quote Post by Shwiggie » Fri Aug 25, 2006 11:22 pm zeaven wrote: Maybe all that stuff was discussed long ago and everyone is bored of it now....

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sat Aug 26, 2006 8:22 am shhhhhh...your not supposed to tell them that....its all they have to live for.....dont take that from them

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 26, 2006 12:26 pm master0rolando wrote: First....look at Shinji......no self confidence, whines, wants everyone to be nice to him, fears rejection, questions his worth.....BOY Now a MAN on the other hand.....knows that he is capable of doing good things....isnt afraid to disagree withsomeone even if they hate em for it...if a woman rejects him?...fogetahbudit, he'll get the next one...MAN...

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sat Aug 26, 2006 12:31 pm yes...the difference between girl and woman is pretty much the same too... hence how...frankly...and a little disturbingly...Eva seems to be a twisted, gigantic, figurative representation of a boy and a girl going through puberty

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Aug 27, 2006 8:55 am NOTE: this post was ment to be posted after Helios', but my net connection got fucked up, so I saved it and so its here. Indeed. But its no wonder that there aren't too many - if any - discussions about this. Or at least I'm not surprised. What many of us like - or at least I think so - in NGE is that it won't tell you what to do. Its rather a slam on your mind and heart to shake you up from your slumber. And after analising NGE into tiny little bits of effectively useless information, some of them lay back into that slumber, with the memory of the shock they experienced. Some of them don't . As you may have found out, NGE is about lonely introverts (two phrases that tend to be a pair as I see), who try to find a reason to breathe. And this reason is different for all of the characters - the case is the same with the viewers. Who tend to find that "Hey, that Shinji bro is just like I am". But they can see others' point of view, so they can obsevre their situation from above, knowing of every action and reaction, reason of the action and the reaction. And when all the info is between their ears, they tend to find that "Hey, this world isn't that fucked up, and the people around me have their own lives, and not I am the only feeling person on this mudball... I mean, planet." This is how I saw things. This is my truth, not yours, so don't think that I've explained anything. I've just told my experience, my truth. BTW I was talking about both the viewer and the characters. *** *** *** Well, the sentence "be a man" is equal with "be strong", though I have an urge to scream that the words "man" and "strong" have nothing to do with each other. Men were useful. Women were useful. But now? Look around when you're walking somewhere in the/a city: all you see is people m-/d-ating. And Anno is not different, not to mention some viewers. He is an introvert, and all an introvert can do when mating comes to his mind is to fwap-fwap all around, since there is absolutely nobody whom he could lay with, due to his introversion. And of course he is full of angst... since unburnt/unused sexual energy tends to turn into anger or sadness->depression. "Be strong"... sheesh... what a joke... like a blind man saying to another one that the sky is so blue... -_- When I hear this, I always wonder that just who is s/he trying to convince? Me, or him/herself?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sun Aug 27, 2006 11:58 pm The thing is that discussing the plot can be "positive" as in empiric. There's a correct and an incorrect that works for everyone. Things that are in one way and never in other. But the discussion about the morals of the series is something subjective where there isn't really much of a point in discussing. I believe that part, the moral one in the Eva message, is something you have to work on your own and keep to thyself.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Mon Aug 28, 2006 3:10 am AchtungAffen wrote: The thing is that discussing the plot can be "positive" as in empiric. There's a correct and an incorrect that works for everyone. Things that are in one way and never in other. But the discussion about the morals of the series is something subjective where there isn't really much of a point in discussing. I believe that part, the moral one in the Eva message, is something you have to work on your own and keep to thyself.

's kind of interesting to me how everyone seems to be interested in figuring out the particular details behind the plot points and battles and etc. in Evangelion instead of concentrating on the points Anno was trying to make and realizing that everything else was just a means to that end. Do people only like Eva for the compelling plot and not the introspective nature of it? Well, I know that's not the case, but that's certainly all the discussion I've seen. Is Anno's psychological message old hat? Has everyone settled on just everything he wanted to say? Do we not care about the themes and styles and techniques present in the anime and just focus on the particulars instead?

Article: A meta observation
 that I've explained anything. I've just told my experience, my truth. BTW I was talking about both the viewer and the characters. *** *** *** Well, the sentence "be a man" is equal with "be strong", though I have an urge to scream that the words "man" and "strong" have nothing to do with each other. Men were useful. Women were useful. But now? Look around when you're walking somewhere in the/a city: all you see is people m-/d-ating. And Anno is not different, not to mention some viewers. He is an introvert, and all an introvert can do when mating comes to his mind is to fwap-fwap all around, since there is absolutely nobody whom he could lay with, due to his introversion. And of course he is full of angst... since unburnt/unused sexual energy tends to turn into anger or sadness->depression. "Be strong"... sheesh... what a joke... like a blind man saying to another one that the sky is so blue... -_- When I hear this, I always wonder that just who is s/he trying to convince? Me, or him/herself?
